title: Evaluate snapshot-based indexd search outside the shared store lock
goal: Move indexd exact-search ranking and snippet materialization outside the shared mutable store lock by taking a bounded immutable namespace snapshot, while preserving one-request consistency, deterministic ranking, persistence compatibility and measured memory limits.

acceptance.assertion: The read lock is held only long enough to clone one immutable namespace Arc and read dimensionality; snapshot capture is O(1) and does not clone keys, vectors, metadata or one pointer per entry.
Dot-product ranking, deterministic tie ordering and snippet materialization execute after the shared store read-lock has been released.
Each search observes one internally consistent namespace snapshot even when concurrent upsert or delete operations replace live entries after snapshot capture.
Public search errors, dimensionality behavior, zero-query ordering, result scores, snippets and JSONL persistence remain compatible with the pre-change implementation.

The commit-bound real-workload benchmark shows a material writer lock-wait reduction on medium and large standard scenarios; search latency and allocation changes are reported and any regression budget breach blocks merge.

Per-search snapshot memory is O(1). An overlapping writer may shallow-clone O(namespace entries) key/index and Arc-pointer storage once, but embeddings and metadata remain shared and no snapshot registry is retained.
Focused concurrency and persistence regressions, full workspace tests, Clippy, benchmark contract checks and exact-head GitHub checks pass before merge.
The task does not establish production capacity, ANN readiness, lock-free writes, or correctness beyond the tested exact-search and persistence contracts.

does_not_establish: cross-host comparability without a controlled baseline
metadata.design_boundary: O(1) Arc-backed immutable namespace snapshot; contiguous exact-search entries plus key index; shallow copy-on-write only for an overlapping writer; no global snapshot registry and no embedding or metadata deep clone.
validation: cargo test --workspace --all-features --locked passed on exact head 2b42a5339bd90cb8ffe1519967fd51113be70a1a.
cargo clippy --locked -p indexd --bench indexd_real_workload -- -D warnings passed.
Changed-file rustfmt --check and git diff --check passed.
Snapshot consistency across update and delete, Arc reuse under copy-on-write, lock release with a retained snapshot, HTTP search, migration and persistence roundtrips passed.
Fail-closed benchmark cases rejected insufficient writer improvement, unsupported lock transition and report schema v1 after writing a report.
All eight GitHub checks on PR #261 succeeded before the SHA-bound squash merge.

verified_acceptance.bounded-namespace-snapshot: API captures one Arc<NamespaceItems> under a short read lock; the focused lock-release test acquires a write lock while the snapshot remains retained.
verified_acceptance.ranking-outside-lock: The production handler releases state.store.read() before spawn_blocking exact ranking and snippet materialization.
verified_acceptance.request-snapshot-consistency: Focused tests prove original, post-update and post-delete snapshots retain their own internally consistent entries and snippets.
verified_acceptance.behavior-and-persistence-compatible: Full workspace, HTTP, exact-search, dimensionality, zero-query, migration and JSONL roundtrip tests passed; reviewed head and merge trees are identical.
verified_acceptance.measured-contention-improvement: Controlled same-host v2 A/B passed all budgets. Writer lock-wait p95 improved by more than 99.99% and writer end-to-end maximum by 82.6% to 93.5% across standard scenarios.
verified_acceptance.bounded-memory-contract: Snapshot capture clones one namespace Arc. Copy-on-write tests prove unchanged StoredItem Arcs are reused; only namespace index and pointer storage are shallow-cloned for an overlapping writer.
verified_acceptance.repository-gates: Exact-head local gates and all eight GitHub checks passed before merge.
verified_acceptance.explicit-nonclaims: Task and performance documentation exclude production capacity, production latency, ANN readiness, lock-free writes, zero-cost overlapping updates and uncontrolled cross-host comparison.

metadata.outcome: SemantAH PR #261 moved exact ranking and snippet materialization outside the shared store lock using O(1) immutable namespace snapshots. Controlled A/B evidence shows lower search latency and near-elimination of writer blocking while retaining deterministic exact ranking and the JSONL persistence contract.
